column1: title#shloka_in_sanskrit#transliteration_en#translation_en#word_by_word_en

BG 12.1#अर्जुन उवाच
एवं सततयुक्ता ये भक्तास्त्वां पर्युपासते
ये चाप्यक्षरमव्यक्तं तेषां के योगवित्तमा:#arjuna uvācha
evaṁ satata-yuktā ye bhaktās tvāṁ paryupāsate
ye chāpy akṣharam avyaktaṁ teṣhāṁ ke yoga-vittamāḥ#Arjun inquired: Between those who are steadfastly devoted to Your personal form and those who worship the formless Brahman, whom do You consider to be more perfect in Yog?#arjunaḥ uvācha—Arjun said

BG 12.2#श्रीभगवानुवाच
मय्यावेश्य मनो ये मां नित्ययुक्ता उपासते
श्रद्धया परयोपेतास्ते मे युक्ततमा मता:#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha
mayy āveśhya mano ye māṁ nitya-yuktā upāsate
śhraddhayā parayopetās te me yuktatamā matāḥ#The Lord said: Those who fix their minds on Me and always engage in My devotion with steadfast faith, I consider them to be the best yogis.#śhrī-bhagavān uvācha—the Lord said

BG 12.3#ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते
सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यञ्च कूटस्थमचलन्ध्रुवम्
सन्नियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धय:
ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रता:#ye tv akṣharam anirdeśhyam avyaktaṁ paryupāsate
sarvatra-gam achintyañcha kūṭa-stham achalandhruvam
sanniyamyendriya-grāmaṁ sarvatra sama-buddhayaḥ
te prāpnuvanti mām eva sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ#BG 12.3-4:#ye—who
column21: sarva-bhūta-hite—in the welfare of all beings

column1:
BG 12.4#ये त्वक्षरमनिर्देश्यमव्यक्तं पर्युपासते
सर्वत्रगमचिन्त्यञ्च कूटस्थमचलन्ध्रुवम्
सन्नियम्येन्द्रियग्रामं सर्वत्र समबुद्धय:
ते प्राप्नुवन्ति मामेव सर्वभूतहिते रता:#ye tv akṣharam anirdeśhyam avyaktaṁ paryupāsate
sarvatra-gam achintyañcha kūṭa-stham achalandhruvam
sanniyamyendriya-grāmaṁ sarvatra sama-buddhayaḥ
te prāpnuvanti mām eva sarva-bhūta-hite ratāḥ#BG 12.3-4:#ye—who
column21: sarva-bhūta-hite—in the welfare of all beings

column1: BG 12.5#क्लेशोऽधिकतरस्तेषामव्यक्तासक्तचेतसाम् |\nअव्यक्ता हि गतिर्दु:खं देहवद्भिरवाप्यते#kleśho ’dhikataras teṣhām avyaktāsakta-chetasām\navyaktā hi gatir duḥkhaṁ dehavadbhir avāpyate#For those whose minds are attached to the unmanifest, the path of realization is full of tribulations. Worship of the unmanifest is exceedingly difficult for embodied beings.#kleśhaḥ—tribulations

BG 12.6#ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्न्यस्य मत्परा:
अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते
तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात्
भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम्#ye tu sarvāṇi karmāṇi mayi sannyasya mat-parāḥ
ananyenaiva yogena māṁ dhyāyanta upāsate
teṣhām ahaṁ samuddhartā mṛityu-saṁsāra-sāgarāt
bhavāmi na chirāt pārtha mayy āveśhita-chetasām#BG 12.6-7:#ye—who
column7: mat-parāḥ—regarding Me as the Supreme goal
column17: mṛityu-saṁsāra-sāgarāt—from the ocean of birth and death
column23: āveśhita chetasām—of those whose consciousness is united

column1:
BG 12.7#ये तु सर्वाणि कर्माणि मयि संन्न्यस्य मत्परा:
अनन्येनैव योगेन मां ध्यायन्त उपासते
तेषामहं समुद्धर्ता मृत्युसंसारसागरात्
भवामि नचिरात्पार्थ मय्यावेशितचेतसाम्#ye tu sarvāṇi karmāṇi mayi sannyasya mat-parāḥ
ananyenaiva yogena māṁ dhyāyanta upāsate
teṣhām ahaṁ samuddhartā mṛityu-saṁsāra-sāgarāt
bhavāmi na chirāt pārtha mayy āveśhita-chetasām#BG 12.6-7:#ye—who
column7: mat-parāḥ—regarding Me as the Supreme goal
column17: mṛityu-saṁsāra-sāgarāt—from the ocean of birth and death
column23: āveśhita chetasām—of those whose consciousness is united

column1: BG 12.8#मय्येव मन आधत्स्व मयि बुद्धिं निवेशय\nनिवसिष्यसि मय्येव अत ऊर्ध्वं न संशय:#mayy eva mana ādhatsva mayi buddhiṁ niveśhaya\nnivasiṣhyasi mayy eva ata ūrdhvaṁ na sanśhayaḥ#Fix your mind on Me alone and surrender your intellect to Me. There upon, you will always live in Me. Of this, there is no doubt.#mayi—on Me

BG 12.9#अथ चित्तं समाधातुं न शक्नोषि मयि स्थिरम्\nअभ्यासयोगेन ततो मामिच्छाप्तुं धनञ्जय#atha chittaṁ samādhātuṁ na śhaknoṣhi mayi sthiram\nabhyāsa-yogena tato mām ichchhāptuṁ dhanañjaya#If you are unable to fix your mind steadily on Me, O Arjun, then practice remembering Me with devotion while constantly restraining the mind from worldly affairs.#atha—if
column7: abhyāsa-yogena—by uniting with God through repeated practice

column1: BG 12.10#अभ्यासेऽप्यसमर्थोऽसि मत्कर्मपरमो भव\nमदर्थमपि कर्माणि कुर्वन्सिद्धिमवाप्स्यसि#abhyāse ’py asamartho ’si mat-karma-paramo bhava\nmad-artham api karmāṇi kurvan siddhim avāpsyasi#If you cannot practice remembering Me with devotion, then just try to work for Me. Thus performing devotional service to Me, you shall achieve the stage of perfection.#abhyāse—in practice

BG 12.11#अथैतदप्यशक्तोऽसि कर्तुं मद्योगमाश्रित:\nसर्वकर्मफलत्यागं तत: कुरु यतात्मवान्#athaitad apy aśhakto ’si kartuṁ mad-yogam āśhritaḥ\nsarva-karma-phala-tyāgaṁ tataḥ kuru yatātmavān#If you are unable to even work for Me in devotion, then try to renounce the fruits of your actions and be situated in the self.#atha—if

BG 12.12#श्रेयो हि ज्ञानमभ्यासाज्ज्ञानाद्ध्यानं विशिष्यते\nध्यानात्कर्मफलत्यागस्त्यागाच्छान्तिरनन्तरम्#śhreyo hi jñānam abhyāsāj jñānād dhyānaṁ viśhiṣhyate\ndhyānāt karma-phala-tyāgas tyāgāch chhāntir anantaram#Better than mechanical practice is knowledge
column2: better than knowledge is meditation. Better than meditation is renunciation of the fruits of actions, for peace immediately follows such renunciation.#śhreyaḥ—better
column10: karma-phala-tyāgaḥ—renunciation of the fruits of actions

column1:
BG 12.13#अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्र: करुण एव च
निर्ममो निरहङ्कार: समदु:खसुख: क्षमी
सन्तुष्ट: सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चय:
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्त: स मे प्रिय:#adveṣhṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva cha
nirmamo nirahankāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣhamīsantuṣhṭaḥ satataṁ yogī yatātmā dṛiḍha-niśhchayaḥ
mayy arpita-mano-buddhir yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ#BG 12.13-14:#adveṣhṭā—free from malice

BG 12.14#अद्वेष्टा सर्वभूतानां मैत्र: करुण एव च
निर्ममो निरहङ्कार: समदु:खसुख: क्षमी
सन्तुष्ट: सततं योगी यतात्मा दृढनिश्चय:
मय्यर्पितमनोबुद्धिर्यो मद्भक्त: स मे प्रिय:#adveṣhṭā sarva-bhūtānāṁ maitraḥ karuṇa eva cha
nirmamo nirahankāraḥ sama-duḥkha-sukhaḥ kṣhamīsantuṣhṭaḥ satataṁ yogī yatātmā dṛiḍha-niśhchayaḥ
mayy arpita-mano-buddhir yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ#BG 12.13-14:#adveṣhṭā—free from malice

BG 12.15#यस्मान्नोद्विजते लोको लोकान्नोद्विजते च य:\nहर्षामर्षभयोद्वेगैर्मुक्तो य: स च मे प्रिय:#yasmān nodvijate loko lokān nodvijate cha yaḥ\nharṣhāmarṣha-bhayodvegair mukto yaḥ sa cha me priyaḥ#Those who are not a source of annoyance to anyone and who in turn are not agitated by anyone, who are equal in pleasure and pain, and free from fear and anxiety, such devotees of Mine are very dear to Me.#yasmāt—by whom

BG 12.16#अनपेक्ष: शुचिर्दक्ष उदासीनो गतव्यथ:\nसर्वारम्भपरित्यागी यो मद्भक्त: स मे प्रिय:#anapekṣhaḥ śhuchir dakṣha udāsīno gata-vyathaḥ\nsarvārambha-parityāgī yo mad-bhaktaḥ sa me priyaḥ#Those who are indifferent to worldly gains, externally and internally pure, skillful, without cares, untroubled, and free from selfishness in all undertakings, such devotees of Mine are very dear to Me.#anapekṣhaḥ—indifferent to worldly gain

BG 12.17#यो न हृष्यति न द्वेष्टि न शोचति न काङ् क्षति\nशुभाशुभपरित्यागी भक्तिमान्य: स मे प्रिय:#yo na hṛiṣhyati na dveṣhṭi na śhochati na kāṅkṣhati\nśhubhāśhubha-parityāgī bhaktimān yaḥ sa me priyaḥ#Those who neither rejoice in mundane pleasures nor despair in worldly sorrows, who neither lament for any loss nor hanker for any gain, who renounce both good and evil deeds, such persons who are full of devotion are very dear to Me.#yaḥ—who
column10: śhubha-aśhubha-parityāgī—who renounce both good and evil deeds

column1:
BG 12.18#सम: शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयो:
शीतोष्णसुखदु:खेषु सम: सङ्गविवर्जित:
तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित्
अनिकेत: स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नर:#samaḥ śhatrau cha mitre cha tathā mānāpamānayoḥ
śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣhu samaḥ saṅga-vivarjitaḥ
tulya-nindā-stutir maunī santuṣhṭo yena kenachit
aniketaḥ sthira-matir bhaktimān me priyo naraḥ#BG 12.18-19:#samaḥ—alike
column16: aniketaḥ—without attachment to the place of residence

column1:
BG 12.19#सम: शत्रौ च मित्रे च तथा मानापमानयो:
शीतोष्णसुखदु:खेषु सम: सङ्गविवर्जित:
तुल्यनिन्दास्तुतिर्मौनी सन्तुष्टो येन केनचित्
अनिकेत: स्थिरमतिर्भक्तिमान्मे प्रियो नर:#samaḥ śhatrau cha mitre cha tathā mānāpamānayoḥ
śhītoṣhṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣhu samaḥ saṅga-vivarjitaḥ
tulya-nindā-stutir maunī santuṣhṭo yena kenachit
aniketaḥ sthira-matir bhaktimān me priyo naraḥ#BG 12.18-19:#samaḥ—alike
column16: aniketaḥ—without attachment to the place of residence

column1: BG 12.20#ये तु धर्म्यामृतमिदं यथोक्तं पर्युपासते\nश्रद्दधाना मत्परमा भक्तास्तेऽतीव मे प्रिया:#ye tu dharmyāmṛitam idaṁ yathoktaṁ paryupāsate\nśhraddadhānā mat-paramā bhaktās te ’tīva me priyāḥ#Those who honor this nectar of wisdom declared here, have faith in Me, and are devoted and intent on Me as the supreme goal, they are exceedingly dear to Me.#ye—who
column10: mat-paramāḥ—intent on Me as the supreme goal
